purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth stack where an error is returned if HCI_UNREGISTER flag has been set. This flag is set when hci_unregister_dev is called which could lead to use-after-free (UAF) errors after the timeout as the hdev will be freed.
The commit removes an unnecessary 'asm/termios.h' include in 'n_hdlc.c'. This is a perfective change as it improves the code quality by removing unnecessary includes.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' in store operations. It does not change any code or configuration files.

The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb module of the Linux kernel by updating the open vm_ops to allocate a new vma lock for the new vma during splitting. It also prevents bugs by ensuring that a vma lock is present for all VM_MAYSHARE vmas.
The commit adds two new power supply names to the dt-bindings for the Realtek rt5682s codec. These are AVDD and MICVDD, which were already used in sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi.

The commit fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one(). The error handling path of the probe releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function. In some cases, an ioremap() must be undone. This is done by adding an iounmap() call in the remove function.
The commit message indicates that a field named 'work' in the struct 'mlx5e_async_ctx' is not used. The purpose of this commit seems to be removing this unused field.
The commit is changing a struct member from unsigned to signed in order to avoid a warning from static checkers such as smatch. This change seems to be related to handling authentication keys.
The commit message suggests that this change is a sorting operation for CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS. It's not clear what the functional changes are, but it seems to be related to configuration files.

This commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open coded check for CAN-FD mode. This function is used in the driver code to determine if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.

The commit message suggests that there is no point in failing the tests when RTC (Real Time Clock) is not present. This implies that the code changes are related to handling or checking for the presence of a Real Time Clock.

The commit is about removing a redundant assignment to variable 'bytes'. The variable 'bytes' is being assigned a value that is never read. This assignment can be removed without affecting the functionality of the code.

The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks. This is done by removing a check that determines whether to include RCU Tasks in the kernel based on the presence of rcuscale.
The commit message suggests that a duplicated phrase 'be split' in spte.h has been removed. The change is likely to be a bug-fixing commit as it fixes a bug present in the source code.

The commit reverts a change in the AMD display driver that populates a clock table based on DCFCLK states. The purpose of this change is to prevent an underflow situation when DCFCLK states are disabled and PMFW fills out the table using DCFCLK based clocks.
The commit adds a new string to the dt-bindings for the Mali Bifrost GPU in the RZ/V2L SoC. This is a perfective commit as it improves the quality of the code by adding documentation.

The commit renames a struct from 'pcie_port' to 'dw_pcie_rp'. This is done to make the naming consistent across all entities in the DW PCIe core driver. The reasoning behind this change is to distinguish between local and common PCIe name spaces, and to use '_ep' suffix for endpoint-related entities.

The commit fixes a clang warning related to formatting in the xgbe-drv.c file. The warning is about using '%#06hx' format specifier with an 'int' argument, which can lead to incorrect output. The commit changes this to use '%#06x' instead.

The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture support for jump labels. The bug is related to branch range checking, which was causing a warning due to an incorrect comparison of signed and unsigned values. The fix involves casting the upper bound of the branch range to long, which avoids triggering this warning.

The commit message indicates that a function is called in the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case of SetHwReg8188EU(). The function call is removed from this case. This suggests that the function is being used to handle some hardware-related task, possibly related to offloading.
The commit declares a symbol as static in arm64 architecture. The sparse warning is about a non-global symbol not being declared as static.

The commit is about removing an incorrect interrupt flags cell from the MDSS nodes in dt-bindings display/msm. The purpose of this change is to correct a mistake in the interrupt provider which is a single cell.
The commit message suggests that it is allocating space for arrays in a struct called ctrl_ref. This could be related to allowing drivers to change the array size from within the driver.
The commit adds new PMICs (Power Management IC) and SoCs (System on Chip) to a socinfo driver. The driver is used for managing power management in the system.

The commit recognizes all possible chip cuts from A to P (15). It does this by printing 'unknown' when the chip cut is greater than E. The commit also includes a patch that modifies the print statement for unknown chip cuts.

The commit message suggests that a software enable count is being dropped from PRCMU clocks in the ux500 core. This could potentially be related to bug fixing or perfective commits as it's about improving code quality and performance.

The commit is about refactoring code to improve readability and maintainability. It moves a macro used in XIP kernel case to be adjacent to the #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directive and groups first type usage cases into one.

This commit enables QUP (Quick Universal Protocol) and GPI DMA (General Purpose Input Output Direct Memory Access) hardware in the sm6350-lena device. It seems to be related to I2C nodes which are used for inter-integrated circuit communication.
The commit is a bug-fixing commit as it fixes a typo in comments and improves coding style.

The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function. The infinite loop may occur if we fail to acquire the HW semaphore, which is needed for resource release. This will typically happen if the hardware is surprise-removed. At this stage there is nothing to do, except log an error and quit.
This commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use more modern terminology for clocking. It removes old style defines for bus clocks.
The commit is removing a pointless hack that was used to check if a file was a swap file. The code checks for the IS_SWAPFILE macro which is always defined in this context.

The commit implements a GTP echo response mechanism for handling GTP messages. It involves sending an echo response to any incoming GTP packet with the message type of GTP_ECHO_REQ. The echo response includes information elements with GTPIE_RECOVERY tag and restart counter value.

The commit message describes a patch that replaces 'next' versions of cpumask functions with 'first' versions where appropriate. The 'first' version is more effective than the 'next' version if n == -1 (which means start == 0). There's no 'cpumask_first_zero()' function, so this patch creates it.

The commit is about removing a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in brcmfmac module. This call was made when the STA roams from one AP to another and it tries to get TIM information from firmware. However, this functionality is no longer supported in the firmware, so the call will always fail. The commit changes ensure that the host driver will no longer try to get TIM information from firmware.

The commit adds support for a specific codec (Realtek RT5682) on a platform without an amplifier. It does this by connecting the codec to SSP0, which is a component of Intel's SoC (System on Chip). The commit also includes a new board configuration for this setup.

The commit is about improving the io_uring/net module in Linux kernel by handling zerocopy sendmsg requests for protocols that don't support it. It fails such requests and does the same for zerocopy sendmsg.

The commit is about improving the situation by testing existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it. It's a perfective commit (PRC) as it improves the quality of the code and doesn't fix any bugs or add new features.

The commit is adding a new macro definition to a header file in the Linux kernel. The macro is used for handling specific cases in a switch statement. It's not clear if this macro will be used for bug fixing or feature enhancement.

The commit message indicates that it replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in drm/simpledrm. The replacement is done to improve the architecture of the code and allow for easier code sharing among drivers. No functional changes are mentioned.

The commit message suggests that a new interface for JPEG decoding worker has been added to support parallel execution of three hardware units in the MT8195. This is likely related to multimedia processing.

The commit message indicates that it is removing pointless VPRINTK() calls from the libata codebase. It also mentions removing ata_scsi_dump_cdb(), which seems to be a function related to SCSI tracing. The commit also includes two sign-offs, indicating that it has been reviewed by Hannes Reinecke and Damien Le Moal.

The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD's display driver. It involves changes to how the driver handles Freesync state transitions, specifically when entering and exiting this state.

The commit fixes a bug in the AMDKFD (Kernel-mode Driver Framework for Direct Rendering Manager) module. It updates the function pointer prototype of hqd_destroy() callback to match reality so that there is no more Control Flow Integrity (CFI) violation.

The commit corrects a typo in the 'resets' property name in the dt-bindings for stm32. It also adds missing 'additionalProperties'. The purpose of this commit is to fix an error that didn't show up due to missing 'additionalProperties: false', which would have prevented the typo from causing a problem.
The commit is about silencing a GCC warning related to array bounds. It's not adding any new features or fixing bugs, but it does improve the code quality by removing warnings.
The commit is changing the code to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate function.
The commit is about splitting out fixed file installation and removal into a separate module from the filetable code. It also involves changing how helpers handle context instead of io_kiocb.
The commit updates the device tree bindings for two separate decoder blocks in the NXP IMX8MQ VPU. It adds support for these blocks to be independently controlled by a power-domain controller.
The commit is removing a macro named IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON from the iwlwifi codebase. This change does not seem to be related to any bug fixes or new features.
